contraventionDescription: Failing to comply with a no entry sign. (Contravention Code 51)

sections.heading: Failing to comply with a no entry sign. (Contravention Code 51)
sections.content: Local authorities are now enforcing this contravention using CCTV enforcement.
sections.contentHtml: Local authorities are now enforcing this contravention using CCTV enforcement.

sections.heading: How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
sections.content: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.
sections.contentHtml: We may be able to assist you in having your penalty charge notice for this contravention cancelled.

sections.heading: Follow our 3 stage process:
sections.content:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)
2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain
3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions
sections.contentHtml:
1 Check: Specific grounds for appeal for this contravention (below on this page)

2 Check: What a Penalty Charge Notice must contain

3 Check: Statutory grounds for appeal for Moving Traffic Contraventions

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.content:
As the contravention is that of “failing to comply with a no entry sign,” it is important to remember that in order to prove this contravention that the enforcing authority must actually show you passing the sign. It is not sufficient to simply show you in or entering a one way street. You can ask to see the video or photographic evidence. This only applies if the words “Failing to comply with a no entry sign” are present. However regardless of this the enforcing authority must prove that the signs were clear and compliant on the day of the contravention in order to enforce this.
There are 6 more ways that you can have your penalty charge notice cancelled for this offence. We have even summarised adjudicator’s decisions where motorists have won.
Go to top
The no entry signs must be placed on each side of the road unless the road is less than 5 meters wide. It is very rare that a road is less than 5 meters wide. If there is only one sign and the road was not less than 5 metes wide then the contravention is not enforceable
Are the signs the correct size (see diagrams above)
Were there any other signs or road markings that contradict the no entry restriction? For example we have seen on occasion a road signed as no entry but the road markings show it as a 2 way road.
In one case there were bollards along the middle of the road with arrows warning motorists to pass to the left of the bollards even through the road was one way against the arrows.
If there are restrictions at certain times were times stated on the Penalty charge notice (PCN) actually the same as on the signs.
Also were there signs warning of CCTV camera enforcement in the vicinity. Whilst these are not compulsory under the traffic management act they are compulsory under the guidlines issued by the information commissioner’s office (ICO)There is no guarantee however that an adjudicator would rule in your favour as we had a case where he did not. although in another case the adjudicator did!
Remember that you can cite compassionate grounds as a reason for an appeal against a penalty charge notice.
Now take a look at the adjudicators decisions for this contravention.
Back to Moving Traffic
